agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Codex 0.130.0 ships a genuine Claude-Code-style **Plan Mode** — a *collaboration mode*, not a command-approval policy. It is entered interactively with the `/plan` slash command (present in the native binary's slash registry alongside `/clear`, `/compact`, `/model`, `/status`). Once in Plan Mode the session carries `collaboration_mode.mode = "plan"` (visible on `turn_context` / `task_started.collaboration_mode_kind`) and a dedicated developer prompt titled `# Plan Mode (Conversational)`: "You are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it... You must not perform **mutating** actions... When you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially." The prompt is explicit that the plan is a review gate: "The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block... Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan." That is a plan-drafts-then-blocks-on-user-decision gate — distinct from codex's `--ask-for-approval` command-approval policy (a per-command sandbox prompt, NOT plan mode).

This is observed empirically, not just in docs: two real local rollouts captured under codex 0.130.0 (`019e1d3e-…` 2026-05-12 and `019e2c55-…` 2026-05-15, both `originator: codex-tui`) contain assistant `final_answer` messages wrapping a real `<proposed_plan>…</proposed_plan>` block, immediately followed by `task_complete` with the turn ending and no further agent activity until the user replies.

## How irrlicht observes it

The Source variant is `FilesUnderRoot{~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser}`. The codex parser (parser.go `parseCodexMessage`, lines 192–199) detects a *complete* `<proposed_plan>…</proposed_plan>` span inside an **assistant** `text`/`output_text` content block (role-gated: a developer-prompt mention does NOT fire — `assistantContentContainsBlock` + the developer-role guard) and synthesizes a tool_use `{ID: "codex-proposed-plan", Name: "ExitPlanMode"}`. That open tool call survives the subsequent `task_complete` (mapped to `turn_done`): the tailer carries `HasOpenToolCall = true` with `ExitPlanMode` in `LastOpenToolNames`, so the state classifier routes the session to **waiting** rather than ready even though the turn is "done." This is exactly the same waiting branch claudecode reaches via a real ExitPlanMode tool_use. It is verified by unit tests `TestParser_ProposedPlan_SynthesizesExitPlanMode`, `TestParser_ProposedPlan_EndToEndWaitingState` (HasOpenToolCall=true after proposed_plan + task_complete), `TestParser_ProposedPlan_DeveloperMessageDoesNotTrigger`, `TestParser_ProposedPlan_PartialTagDoesNotTrigger`, and the dedupe/close-by-user-reply tests, plus the committed regression fixture `replaydata/agents/codex/regression/proposed-plan-pending/`. On the user's next prompt the synthetic ExitPlanMode is cleared (`ClearToolNames` on the user message) so the session recovers waiting→working→ready — the full approval arc.

Observability caveat: codex Plan Mode is **TUI-only**. There is no `codex exec` (headless) flag and no `-c` config key to start a session in plan mode (confirmed against `codex --help`, `codex exec --help`, and the feature-flag registry — `collaboration_modes` shows as a removed flag, but the `/plan` runtime command is live). The recipe therefore drives the interactive REPL (`drive-codex-interactive.sh`, `codex --no-alt-screen`), entering plan mode via the `/plan` slash step before prompting. Despite the scenario's `requires: [headless_mode]` tag, the *observable lifecycle* (working→waiting→working→ready on one live rollout) is identical to the claudecode arc; the only difference is the entry channel (interactive `/plan` vs claudecode's headless `permissions.defaultMode=plan`).

## Capability key

Primitive exercised = `plan_mode`. codex/capabilities.json currently marks `plan_mode: "unknown"`; this assessment supports flipping it to `"yes"` (the parent updates capabilities.json centrally).
caveats: Plan Mode is TUI-only — there is no headless `codex exec` entry point and no `-c collaboration_mode=plan` config key (codex 0.130.0). The recipe must drive the interactive REPL and enter via the `/plan` slash command, unlike claudecode's headless `permissions.defaultMode=plan`.

The `/plan` slash toggles a mode and does NOT produce a turn (no task_complete), so the recipe uses a sleep — not wait_turn — after the slash; wait_turn would over-count against the EXPECTED_TURNS bump that every send/slash step applies.

Plan Mode's developer prompt encourages a PHASE-1 exploration/clarifying-question pass before emitting the plan; on a real repo the first proposed_plan can be many minutes out. The recipe runs in an empty fresh cwd and prompts for an immediate single-turn `<proposed_plan>` to keep the turn short and deterministic.

The waiting state is driven by a SYNTHETIC ExitPlanMode (codex has no structured ExitPlanMode tool_use); it is the `<proposed_plan>` text span the parser keys on, so a malformed or unclosed tag would not gate.

sources.note: parseCodexMessage lines 192-199 synthesize ExitPlanMode from a complete <proposed_plan>…</proposed_plan> assistant block; assistantContentContainsBlock + developer-role guard
TestParser_ProposedPlan_EndToEndWaitingState — HasOpenToolCall=true with ExitPlanMode after proposed_plan + task_complete; plus Synthesize/DeveloperMessage/PartialTag/Dedupe/ClosedByUserReply tests
Committed regression fixture derived from a real codex plan-mode rollout: collaboration_mode.mode=plan, assistant final_answer with <proposed_plan>, then task_complete

Real codex 0.130.0 TUI rollout — turn_context collaboration_mode.mode=plan with the full '# Plan Mode (Conversational)' developer prompt; assistant final_answer emits a genuine <proposed_plan> block followed by task_complete
user-blocking-plan-mode-approval Primitive exercised names 'Codex <proposed_plan> block' as a valid plan_mode anchor
Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser} — the transcript-tailer path the synthetic ExitPlanMode flows through
OpenAI Codex CLI — Plan Mode is a collaboration mode (verified against codex-cli 0.130.0 binary slash registry: /plan present)

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)
codex 0.130.0+ — Plan Mode is the `/plan` collaboration mode (TUI only; no headless `codex exec` plan entry, no `-c collaboration_mode` config key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
setup: The interactive driver launches `codex --no-alt-screen` in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, accepts the trust dialog, and waits for the TUI banner + MCP boot before the first step

Step 1 is a `/plan` slash that switches the session into Plan Mode. Unlike a prompt, `/plan` toggles a mode and does NOT start a turn (no task_complete), so it is followed by a fixed `sleep`, not `wait_turn` — wait_turn would over-count against the EXPECTED_TURNS bump every send/slash applies and time out.

Plan Mode's developer prompt encourages a PHASE-1 exploration pass; running in an empty cwd plus an explicit 'emit the plan now' instruction keeps the plan turn to a single short reply that wraps a <proposed_plan> block.

The codex parser synthesizes a SYNTHETIC ExitPlanMode tool_use from the <proposed_plan> span (parser.go parseCodexMessage); that open call survives task_complete so the session sits in waiting (the plan-review gate) until the user approves. The interactive driver keeps the rollout session ALIVE across both turns; the REPL is torn down only at script end, so the daemon observes the full waiting→working→ready recovery on one live session.

The approval turn (step 4) MUST do enough real work to span several wall-clock seconds with intermediate tool calls — it tells codex to create a.txt/b.txt/c.txt and run `ls` as distinct steps before its final summary. WHY: the daemon coalesces transcript activity per session in a 2s debounce window (session_detector_activity.go), and a turn-end (Terminal) event short-circuits the window. A trivial text-only recovery reply (the original 'confirm in one sentence' prompt) landed its user message and the agent's whole reply in a single sub-2s burst, so the FIRST activity event already saw a completed turn and the classifier went waiting→ready directly — the brief working blip was never emitted (debounce-coalesced). Spreading the recovery across several tool calls means the first post-approval activity event observes an IN-PROGRESS turn → working, and the terminal task_complete arrives seconds later → ready. That produces the OBSERVABLE waiting→working→ready arc the spec asserts. This is the same longer-interactive-turn fix that made basic-turn capture a distinct working→ready pair. codex runs in its default workspace-write sandbox with approval_policy=on-request, so creating files inside the fresh cwd and running `ls` there are autonomous workspace operations that do not raise a command-approval prompt (which would itself be a different waiting gate). If a future codex default DID gate one of these commands, the recovery arc would show an extra waiting episode — re-check the sandbox/approval config in that case.

script.text: We are in Plan Mode. Do NOT explore the repo and do NOT ask any clarifying questions — there is nothing to explore here. Immediately produce your final plan for this trivial task: add a file hello.py containing a single line `print("hello world")`. Output ONLY a single <proposed_plan> block (opening tag on its own line, closing tag on its own line) with a short Summary, Key Changes, and Test Plan. No other text.

Approved — proceed with implementation now. Do NOT emit another <proposed_plan> block and do NOT ask any questions. Execute this multi-step task in order: (1) create a file a.txt containing the single line `alpha`, (2) create a file b.txt containing the single line `beta`, (3) create a file c.txt containing the single line `gamma`, (4) run `ls` in the working directory, then (5) reply with one short plain sentence summarizing which files now exist. Do the file creations and the `ls` as distinct steps so the work is visible.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created (proc-<PID>) event in ready within 1000 ms of agent launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the rollout session with a distinct session_id, born ready
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the rollout session_id when the plan-request prompt is sent (the first turn after /plan)

events.jsonl contains a state_transition→waiting on the SAME rollout session_id when the agent emits a <proposed_plan> block (synthetic ExitPlanMode keeps a tool call open across task_complete — the plan-review gate; classifier takes the open-user-blocking-tool path rather than →ready)
the waiting episode is visible and persists ≥3s — no state_transition to ready appears before the user approves

events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the SAME rollout session_id when the user approves and asks for implementation (waiting→working recovery; the user message clears the open ExitPlanMode). The recovery turn does real multi-step work (create a.txt/b.txt/c.txt + run ls) so the working span lasts >2s and survives the daemon's debounce window rather than being coalesced into a direct waiting→ready
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→ready on the SAME rollout session_id when the second turn finishes executing the approved task and gives its summary (working→ready)
events.jsonl contains a process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown (single live session across both turns, not per-turn exits)
